steps.title: Identify what proves the professor can't adjust
steps.content: We need the professor's own words expressing discomfort with the country estate—not external judgment of him, not his general background, but his direct reaction to the setting.

steps.title: Match the quotation to the claim
steps.content: Choice C directly shows the professor contrasting his familiar library and lecture hall life with the estate as a “wilderness” of “stupid people,” articulating his fundamental inability to adjust. Choices A, B, and D respectively show external judgment of the professor, his scholarly nature in the abstract, and anxiety about Vanya—none addressing his struggle with the country estate itself. This matches choice C — C is correct.
